notes: an agent-decision predicate records an agent's policy verdict on a tool call (which policies evaluated, allow or deny, the per-call argument commitment, when); it is the attested-decision face of an execution event, the closest foreign analog to a signed decision record

the in-toto Statement is carried in a DSSE envelope whose signature is not modeled here; alg, signature, and receiptAsserted remain a separate signing event, so the predicate declares the decision but does not on its own carry the issuer signature that makes it a receipt assertion

tool_calls[].args_hash commits the call arguments but is not a SEP-2828 backLink, which pins the attestationDigest of an attested request a conformant receipt must answer; the commitment is portable only when args_canonicalization names a shared projection, here JCS (RFC 8785)

args_state carries the explicit-omission state as a first-class attested value (present, args_redacted, args_unavailable, args_not_recorded), so a redacted call and an unrecorded call stay distinct claims rather than collapsing into one missing field
the predicate records the decision (allow or deny the call), not outcomeDerived (whether the call then executed and with what result)

an AP2 payment receipt is the post-settlement final state of an agent payment: a Success or Error status, the issuer (PSP or credentials provider), and on success the psp_confirmation_id and network_confirmation_id that evidence the settled transaction

unlike an x402 settlement or an ACP checkout, an AP2 receipt carries reference: a hash of the closed mandate that authorized the payment, a genuine back-link to what authorized the spend, lifted here to advisory.mandateRef

the receipt is issued as an ES256 SD-JWT-VC, so its signature and alg live in the JWT envelope, not in these decoded claims; a data-only mapping cannot promote a signature it does not carry, so alg and signature are reported missing and the mandate binding stays advisory rather than a proven back-link

the honest upgrade is a signature-verifying profile that validates the SD-JWT and checks reference against the issuer's closed mandate, promoting reference to a SEP-2828 back-link and asserting the receipt signature; that is a computed crypto step, deliberately out of scope for a declarative profile
a C2PA manifest asserts content provenance (who or what created or edited an asset, with which assertions); it is media lineage, not an execution decision-and-outcome
the manifest is sealed into a COSE/DSSE signature at embed time by the signer; the manifest-definition document carries no signature, so alg/signature/receiptAsserted stay a separate event
no SEP-2828 back-link (an attested request) is present
a denial is the outcome of a refused call: it maps to outcomeDerived.status = refused
a refused outcome carries no resultCommitment
authorizationContextId is a correlation handle, not authorization material
the denial carries no completedAt, no signing envelope, and no back-link; the recording side supplies those
a denial is the outcome of a refused call: it maps to outcomeDerived.status = refused
a refused outcome carries no resultCommitment
authorizationContextId is a correlation handle, not authorization material
the denial carries no completedAt, no signing envelope, and no back-link; the recording side supplies those
a denial is the outcome of a refused call: it maps to outcomeDerived.status = refused
a refused outcome carries no resultCommitment
authorizationContextId is a correlation handle, not authorization material
the denial carries no completedAt, no signing envelope, and no back-link; the recording side supplies those
a SEP-2787 attestation is the attested request a SEP-2828 receipt answers; it fixes the exact back-link a conformant receipt must pin
plannerDeclared.intent is client-declared and bound by the issuer's signature, not asserted true by the issuer
the record's own signing (alg, signature, receiptAsserted) is a separate event by the recording side, not derived from the attestation
a SEP-2787 attestation is the attested request a SEP-2828 receipt answers; it fixes the exact back-link a conformant receipt must pin
plannerDeclared.intent is client-declared and bound by the issuer's signature, not asserted true by the issuer
the record's own signing (alg, signature, receiptAsserted) is a separate event by the recording side, not derived from the attestation

advisory.invocationReason: Need to inspect available tables before generating a query.

notes: SEP-2817 is client-asserted input audit context; per its own specification it MUST NOT be used as authorization evidence
it maps to the decision-input plane (the agent's stated intent), the unsigned counterpart of an attested rationale, and populates no required SEP-2828 field
turnId groups requests from one user turn; it is correlation only

advisory.invocationReason: The user asked to view employee rows, and the employees table was identified as the target table.

notes: SEP-2817 is client-asserted input audit context; per its own specification it MUST NOT be used as authorization evidence
it maps to the decision-input plane (the agent's stated intent), the unsigned counterpart of an attested rationale, and populates no required SEP-2828 field
turnId groups requests from one user turn; it is correlation only
a SLSA provenance statement attests how an artifact was built (builder, build type, materials); it is build-time lineage, not an execution decision-and-outcome
the in-toto Statement is carried in a DSSE envelope whose signature is not modeled here; alg/signature/receiptAsserted remain a separate signing event
subject[].digest binds the built artifact, but no SEP-2828 back-link (an attested request) is present
not a SEP-2643 denial, SEP-2787 attestation, or SEP-2817 invocation audit context; nothing to normalize

an x402 settlement response is the settled-transaction face of an agent payment: success or failure, the on-chain transaction hash and CAIP-2 network that anchor it, the payer, and the atomic amount; it is the closest x402 analog to a settled-transaction record

the settlement response is an unsigned server assertion: transaction + network let a verifier independently confirm the payment on-chain, but the response object itself carries no signature over its own bytes, so alg, signature, and receiptAsserted are absent and it states an outcome it does not itself prove

x402 carries no SEP-2828 backLink or outcomeDerived: there is no attestationDigest pinning the payment authorization (the EIP-3009 signed from/to/value/validAfter/validBefore/nonce) or the authorizing decision that a conformant receipt must bind, so the settled amount is reported without a link to what authorized it

the optional offer-receipt extension adds a server EIP-712 signature over {network, resourceUrl, payer, issuedAt, transaction}; when present it is a signed receipt, but its issuedAt is issuance time, not block time, and it is still not the SEP-2828 execution record
